audit_items.proof_trace: In `avl_buildlist`, after `ber_memalloc` (L599) and `ber_memrealloc` (L604-606), the returned pointer is assigned directly to `avl_list` with no NULL check before `avl_list[avl_maxlist++] = data` at L608. Both `ber_memalloc` and `ber_memrealloc` can return NULL on OOM (confirmed by other callers in memory.c that do check). The `ber_memrealloc` failure also leaks the original block. All other dereferences in avl.c are properly NULL-guarded; this is the sole violation.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker who can exhaust slapd/libldap server memory (e.g., via repeated resource-intensive LDAP requests) and then cause a caller of `ldap_avl_getfirst` to invoke `avl_buildlist` on a non-empty tree will trigger a NULL dereference at L608, crashing the process. Within slapd itself `ldap_avl_getfirst` is not called, but the function is exported from libldap and any consuming application is affected.
